situation.headline: Active US-Iran military exchange with strikes on Iranian territory and IRGC retaliatory attacks on US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain marks the most significant direct US-Iran kinetic confrontation in decades, with Iraq's political architecture simultaneously fracturing as Baghdad moves against pro-Iran parliamentarians

domains.name: Middle East
domains.label: regional escalation 90d
domains.status: US-Iran exchange has crossed into direct kinetic threshold with CENTCOM strikes inside Iran, IRGC targeting US Fifth Fleet and Ali Salem Airbase, and Iraqi SOF conducting Green Zone arrests of pro-Iran politicians — escalation ladder is actively being climbed with no ceasefire signal visible

domains.name: Indo-Pacific
domains.label: China coercion escalation
domains.status: US military assets are being drawn hard into Middle East contingencies, reducing INDOPACOM bandwidth and signaling to Beijing a potential window; GDELT shows increased China-Philippines friction and Japanese simulation of Liaoning carrier strike, but no imminent trigger yet

domains.name: Europe
domains.label: NATO cohesion fracture
domains.status: Hungary's Orban ouster and Budapest Pride signal democratic normalization that reduces one fracture point, but OSINT warnings of potential Belarusian front opening against Ukraine and continued Russian strikes on Ukrainian airfields keep European security environment volatile

domains.name: Energy
domains.label: supply shock 60d
domains.status: CENTCOM strikes near Strait of Hormuz and IRGC attacks on Bahrain plus an Iranian drone strike on a Panama-flagged crude tanker KIKU represent direct threats to the roughly 21 million barrels per day transiting Hormuz; a Ukrainian drone strike on the Slavyansk-na-Kubani refinery compounds supply-side pressure

domains.name: US Politics
domains.label: House flip Nov 2026
domains.status: Left-Democratic coalition fractures are visible with Jeffries under pressure from far left, Mamdani momentum signaling progressive insurgency, and a failed Trump-branded spectacle event suggesting MAGA enthusiasm gaps; Beshear 2028 positioning and Ossoff's 13% Kalshi nomination price suggest Democrats see offensive opportunities but coalition coherence remains in question

situation.unseen: The simultaneous Iraqi Green Zone military operation arresting pro-Iran parliamentarians while the US-Iran kinetic exchange is ongoing suggests a coordinated or opportunistically aligned effort by Iraqi Prime Minister al-Zaidi to permanently rebalance Baghdad away from Tehran — this is a potentially decisive structural shift in the Iran proxy network that is being underreported relative to the missile exchange itself, and if successful it collapses a critical node of Iranian regional influence that missiles cannot restore
situation.criticalWindow: The next 72 hours of US-Iran escalation management are the decisive window: IRGC has now conducted two retaliatory waves after US strikes, Iran's Foreign Ministry has declared the MOU violated, and Khamenei's statement about realizing violated rights signals authorization for further escalation; if the US does not establish a credible deterrence ceiling or back-channel communication fast, the Strait of Hormuz closure scenario — which would constitute a global energy shock — moves from tail risk to base case within 30 days
watchFor: Iranian mining or blockade action in the Strait of Hormuz or against additional tanker traffic as a strategic coercion lever against US Gulf partners
Iraqi parliament dissolution or constitutional crisis following Green Zone arrests, which could trigger militia mobilization and threaten US Embassy Baghdad force protection
Chinese naval or air activity in the South China Sea or Taiwan Strait exploiting US CENTCOM preoccupation and reduced carrier strike group availability in INDOPACOM
Belarusian military mobilization toward Ukrainian border as signaled by OSINT, which would force NATO Article 5 consultations and further divide US strategic attention across simultaneous theaters
situation.confidenceNote: Key uncertainties include: the exact terms and status of the violated MOU between US and Iran (scope and who brokered it is unclear from open sources); whether Iraqi PM al-Zaidi is acting with US coordination or independently; actual damage assessment of CENTCOM strikes inside Iran which affects Iranian escalation calculus; and whether Gulf Arab states beyond Bahrain and Kuwait are being drawn in or are remaining neutral — these unknowns could shift the Middle East probability by plus or minus 15 points
situation.topPattern: A compounding strategic crisis is forming where US kinetic engagement with Iran is simultaneously degrading the Iran proxy network in Iraq via Baghdad's Green Zone crackdown, creating a potential decisive but dangerous moment — yet the Strait of Hormuz energy chokepoint exposure and the absence of any active US-Iran diplomatic back-channel visible in open sources means the ladder-climbing dynamic has no visible circuit breaker, while Kalshi prediction markets show zero pricing of near-term Trump removal or political instability, suggesting markets have not yet priced the full tail risk of a prolonged US-Iran war scenario intersecting with a 2026 midterm environment already showing Democratic structural momentum

signals.headline: Iran escalates multi-front missile/drone campaign against US Gulf allies after US retaliatory strikes
signals.summary: Iran's IRGC conducted coordinated drone and missile attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait, targeting US Fifth Fleet HQ and Al-Salem Airbase, followed by renewed volleys after US counter-strikes. Kuwait and Bahrain air defenses intercepted incoming threats with no major US casualties reported, but escalation cycle is active and Trump rhetoric remains maximalist.
signals.signal: Regional deterrence architecture is degrading in real-time. Repeated Iranian salvos despite US retaliation suggest Tehran assesses low cost to continued strikes. Risk of miscalculation or accidental US casualty spike triggering wider war is elevated. Iraqi Green Zone arrests of pro-Iran politicians may signal US-aligned push to decouple Iraq from Iranian orbit, but timing during active Iran-US combat creates instability.
signals.contradicts: Market pricing of Iran conflict risk appears static despite live kinetic exchanges; prediction markets show no Iran escalation premium

signals.headline: White House secretly coordinated to politicize civil service and Trump prepares midterm election rule changes with time running out
signals.summary: Drudge signals WH covertly influenced civil service boards meant to prevent politicization, and Trump is actively pushing through changes to midterm election rules despite tightening timeline. Separate reporting hints at glamorous aide fueling executive delusions and close personal devotion language.
signals.signal: Institutional norms degradation accelerating. Pattern suggests coordinated effort to entrench executive power before potential change of administration or judicial intervention. Midterm rule changes could alter 2026 race dynamics if implemented; civil service politicization will degrade government competence in ongoing crises.
signals.contradicts: Prediction markets show Trump Republican nomination at only 3% despite apparent operational control of party machinery and resources

signals.headline: China resets AI race cyberwar advantage as major tech investor bets against Musk's vision
signals.summary: Beijing is repositioning on AI competitive advantage. Simultaneously, one of tech's largest gamblers is taking shorts against Musk, signaling loss of confidence in Tesla/Neuralink trajectory or broader Musk-led innovation narrative.
signals.signal: US technological edge in AI may be eroding faster than consensus acknowledges. Musk short positioning and China policy reset both suggest market participants see shift in competitive dynamics. This feeds into longer-term US strategic vulnerability if defense-industrial base loses tech leadership.
signals.contradicts: US policy still treats AI leadership as settled; no Kalshi markets on US-China AI parity

signals.headline: Jon Ossoff 2028 Democratic nominee odds jump to 13% and 9% general-election win; Kamala Harris at 8%
signals.summary: Kalshi shows Ossoff gaining ground as Democratic nominee and general-election contender, surpassing Harris. Gavin Newsom (24% nominee) and Rahm Emanuel (5%) split establishment lane; AOC holding at 10% nominee price despite 1-cent decline.
signals.signal: Market is pricing a centrist realignment away from Harris and toward younger, less-defined candidates. Ossoff's rise suggests moderate Democrats see opening with a figure from swing-state background. This contradicts insider betting and suggests grassroots or algorithmic trading driving secondary-tier candidate repricing.
signals.contradicts: No major media narrative on Ossoff surge; insider political betting typically leads markets by weeks

signals.headline: First Budapest Pride march since Orban's removal; tens of thousands participate
signals.summary: Hungary holds large public Pride event for first time post-Orban transition, signaling potential normalization of LGBTQ+ political space and broader democratic reformation under new government.
signals.signal: If sustained, signals EU realignment on democratic norms and human rights. Hungary's pivot away from Orban's illiberal model could reduce one major fracture within European Union. However, timing and durability of new regime uncertain; Orban still commands nationalist support base.
signals.contradicts: No Kalshi market price on Hungary's EU standing or democratic trajectory

signals.headline: Iraq's PM conducts mass arrests of politicians across sectarian lines in Green Zone; US Embassy proximity suggests coordination risk
signals.summary: Iraqi Counter Terrorism Service and Army armor stormed the Green Zone targeting politicians across pro-Iran and Sunni factions. Gunfire reported, multiple arrests including Muthanna al-Samarrai. Timing coincides with Iran-US escalation and suggests possible power consolidation or US-backed crackdown.
signals.signal: Iraq's political stability is fragmenting under pressure from Iran-US competition. If PM is conducting anti-Iran purge with implicit US support, it could provoke Iranian retaliation against US interests in Iraq (embassies, personnel). Alternatively, this signals US trying to surgically decouple Iraq from Iranian orbit during kinetic phase—high-risk operation.
signals.contradicts: No Iraq political crisis pricing in Kalshi; markets treat Iraq as stable

signals.headline: Russia's Slavyansk-na-Kubani refinery burning after Ukrainian drone strike; key missile factory hit
signals.summary: Ukraine conducting deep strikes into Russian energy and military-industrial infrastructure. Refinery fires indicate sustained Ukrainian capability to disrupt Russian fuel supply and defense production despite air-defense saturation efforts.
signals.signal: Ukraine's offensive reach is expanding, not contracting. Russian air defense is leaky or concentrated on other fronts. If energy/arms production degradation accelerates, Russia's ability to sustain Donbas operations or counter-offensive declines, potentially shifting battlefield timeline. Risk of Russian escalation to NATO infrastructure to offset losses.
signals.contradicts: Drudge and OSINT sources show Ukraine offensive momentum, but prediction markets show no shift in Russia-Ukraine war-outcome pricing

scanNote: Signal quality is high on Middle East kinetic action and Iraq political chaos; moderate-to-low on US election market repricing (thin liquidity on secondary candidates); China AI reset is vague headline without operational detail.

markets.label: US Level 4 Taiwan warning before Jul 1 2026
markets.rationale: GDELT Indo-Pacific wires show elevated South China Sea tensions and Japanese carrier simulation reports, but no imminent invasion signal; a Level 4 advisory within days is highly unlikely given current diplomatic posture and the market's own 1% price.

markets.label: Trump buys Greenland before Jul 1 2026
markets.rationale: With the deadline being Jul 1 2026 and no credible acquisition signal in any intel feed — and the market already at 0% — this resolves NO with near certainty.

markets.label: Trump buys Greenland before 2027
markets.rationale: Kazakhstan leader's praise of Trump and the broader pattern of Trump deal-making (mining deal, sons' profits) suggest dealmaking appetite, but no Greenland-specific movement appears in any feed; 7% market price is slightly rich given zero operational indicators.

markets.label: JD Vance Republican 2028 nominee
markets.rationale: Vance as sitting VP is the structural frontrunner, but the Iran-Gulf crisis and domestic governance controversies flagged in Drudge create political volatility that could reshape the 2028 field; market's 43% is approximately fair.

markets.label: Marco Rubio Republican 2028 nominee
markets.rationale: The active Iran-Gulf war scenario elevates Rubio's profile as Secretary of State managing a hot conflict; if the crisis escalates and is handled well, Rubio gains significantly over Vance, making his 29% market price modestly underpriced.

markets.label: Gavin Newsom Democratic 2028 nominee
markets.rationale: CA liberal lawmaker getting heckled at SF Trans March and broader far-left insurgency noted in Drudge (Jeffries test, Mamdani summer) suggest the party's left flank is growing, which structurally disadvantages Newsom; his 24% market price is modestly overpriced.

markets.label: Jon Ossoff Democratic 2028 nominee
markets.rationale: Ossoff has no visible signal in any current intel feed and the broader Democratic field remains wide open; his 13% nomination price appears overpriced relative to name recognition and current political dynamics.

markets.label: Todd Blanche next Attorney General
markets.rationale: Drudge headline about White House secretly swaying the civil service board signals continued executive consolidation of DOJ, consistent with Blanche's trajectory; 72% market price is slightly rich given ongoing institutional instability but directionally correct.

watchSignals: Iran-US escalation cycle: monitor for additional Iranian strikes on Gulf allies (Kuwait, Bahrain) and US response tempo; risk of miscalculation triggering wider conflict
Iraq political crisis: Green Zone arrests of pro-Iran and Sunni politicians signal potential collapse of Baghdad's sectarian balance; watch for militia response and US embassy security implications
Ukraine drone strikes on Russian energy infrastructure: Slavyansk refinery hit confirms degradation of Russian logistics; monitor for Russian escalation toward NATO or Belarus entry
2028 Democratic nominee market: Jon Ossoff (13% win probability) and Gavin Newsom (24% nomination) showing unexpected traction; watch for consolidation as primary season approaches
Trump administration personnel: Todd Blanche 72% probability as next AG; monitor confirmation dynamics and implications for DOJ independence and prosecution strategy
Taiwan and China military signaling: Japan's simulated Liaoning carrier attack and US South China Sea presence noted; watch ASEAN positioning on West Philippine Sea disputes

groundTruth.headline: Iran has conducted multiple waves of drone and ballistic missile attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain targeting US Fifth Fleet and regional bases; US has responded with airstrikes on Iranian targets near Strait of Hormuz
groundTruth.summary: Escalation cycle between US and Iran is active and accelerating. Kuwait intercepted ballistic missiles; Bahrain sustained residential building damage but no US casualties reported. Iran claims 8 infrastructure targets hit; US retaliation ongoing. Risk of further Iranian response is high.
groundTruth.signal: US military posture in Gulf is strained; potential for accidental escalation or Iranian miscalculation increasing. Fifth Fleet operations may face temporary disruption. Regional allies (Gulf States) confidence in US security umbrella being tested in real-time.

groundTruth.headline: Iraqi Special Operations Forces and Army units deployed heavy armor into Baghdad Green Zone conducting arrest operations against politicians including Sunni MP Muthanna al-Samarrai; gunfire reported and complex near US Embassy stormed
groundTruth.summary: Iraqi PM Ali al-Zaidi executing what appears to be coordinated crackdown on both pro-Iran and Sunni political figures, signaling potential breakdown of post-2003 sectarian power-sharing arrangement. Military lockdown of Green Zone unprecedented peacetime action. Legitimacy of arrests across political spectrum unclear.
groundTruth.signal: US Embassy in Baghdad faces unprecedented insider-threat environment. Potential for militia retaliation against Iraqi state forces could destabilize entire country and force US troop posture reassessment. Iraqi state fragmentation risk is acute.

groundTruth.headline: Ukraine conducted successful drone strike on Russia's Slavyansk-na-Kubani oil refinery causing major fire; satellite imagery confirms destruction of Ukrainian MiG-29 fighters at Voznesensk Airfield; reports of potential Belarusian entry into conflict
groundTruth.summary: Ukrainian strike capability against Russian rear-area energy infrastructure is degrading Russian fuel supply chain. Simultaneous loss of advanced fighter aircraft indicates air superiority pressure. Belarus movement toward conflict would open northern front and threaten NATO flank.
groundTruth.signal: Russian military logistics are increasingly vulnerable to long-range Ukrainian strikes. NATO (especially Poland, Baltics) faces potential two-front scenario if Belarus enters. US military aid to Ukraine sustaining offensive capability but attrition rates rising on both sides.

groundTruth.headline: Prediction markets show J.D. Vance at 43% Republican nomination probability; Trump at only 3% win probability in general election despite 3% nomination probability; Democratic field fragmented with Newsom 24%, Ossoff 13% nomination odds
groundTruth.summary: Markets pricing extreme volatility in 2028 race. Trump's near-zero general election win probability despite front-runner status suggests high perceived legal/political risk or market dysfunction. Vance consolidating Republican base. Democratic market shows deep uncertainty about eventual nominee.
groundTruth.signal: Prediction markets indicate structural uncertainty about Trump's viability as general election candidate. Ossoff and Newsom emergence signals Democratic base seeking younger/outsider profiles. 2028 race remains wide open despite current frontrunner positioning.

groundTruth.headline: US strategic presence in South China Sea confirmed active; Japan publicly simulated attack on PLA Navy Liaoning carrier; ASEAN nations internally divided on West Philippine Sea positioning and US alignment
groundTruth.summary: US-Japan military coordination against China increasingly visible and provocative in messaging. ASEAN cohesion fracturing over China policy. Japan's public carrier simulation signals willingness to operationalize Taiwan contingency planning. China response awaited.
groundTruth.signal: US Asia strategy depends on ASEAN fence-sitting; Japanese activism could force earlier alliance consolidation. Taiwan scenario planning is openly occurring. China likely to increase military pressure on Philippines and smaller ASEAN states to break US coalition.
